metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit ADRs 0026–0029 (baseline semantics, ObservedJira states, Decision matrix, circuit breaker) under docs/adr/ on this branch BEFORE any implementation work begins, as the plan text promises.
findings.finding: ADRs 0026–0029 cited as governing specs do not exist at docs/adr/. The existing docs/adr/0026-autodeploy-on-box-timer.md is an unrelated autodeploy ADR; ADRs 0027–0029 are absent entirely.
findings.location: Plan Summary: 'All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST.'
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/0026-per-binding-baseline.md, 0027-decision-matrix.md, 0028-observed-jira-states.md, 0029-circuit-breaker.md with the content the plan refers to (4 decision points for ADR 0026, ABSENT_IN_WINDOW semantics for ADR 0028, etc.). Renumber if the existing 0026 blocks the slot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a section to the plan documenting how classify.py relates to the existing ad-hoc classifier in `outbound_differ.py` (lines 575–620) — specifically whether that logic will be replaced in a follow-on ticket, and whether classify.py's matrix cells are byte-for-byte consistent with the existing branches in the interim.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new classify.py decision matrix that duplicates and partially overlaps the existing ad-hoc absence-classification logic in outbound_differ.py (the `_DELETED` / `_TRANSPORT_ERROR` / 200-alive / retired / deferred branches at lines 575–620). The plan does not reconcile whether classify.py will replace, wrap, or coexist with that logic — leaving two parallel classifiers in the codebase.
findings.location: New module section: 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/classify.py (pure leaf; no I/O, no imports from differ/outbound_differ/inbound_differ)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a per-kind payload schema table to the Implementation Contract specifying the mandatory and optional keys for each DecisionKind (e.g., SYNC_FIELDS: {fields: dict}, TERMINAL_TRANSITION: {target_status: str}, ADOPT: {jira_key: str, fields: dict}, PROBE_GET: {jira_key: str}, RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE: {jira_key: str, absent_count: int}, ALERT: {reason: str}, NOOP: {}).
findings.finding: `Decision.payload: dict` is specified as a field on the Decision dataclass but no per-kind payload schema is defined anywhere in the plan. The test_frozen_census AC asserts 'EXACTLY 7 acting decisions (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION REB-464/456/465/466/457, 1x PROBE_GET REB-530, 1x ADOPT REB-532)' — this requires the fixture tests to inspect Decision values, including their payload, but the payload keys for each DecisionKind are never enumerated. census() also references 'counts-by-kind' but does not need payload; however, the multi-pass test's 'assert self-heal' and the direction-preservation property both depend on payload content (e.g., what field diffs are in SYNC_FIELDS payload).
findings.location: New module section: '@dataclass(frozen=True) class Decision'; Implementation Contract: 'decision_census record shape'; Tests section: 'test_frozen_census.py'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that ADRs 0026-0029 for the reconciler foundation are committed (or will be committed atomically) on this branch, resolve the numbering collision with the existing 0026-autodeploy-on-box-timer.md (renumber either the new or the existing ADR), and update all plan references to use the final ADR numbers.
findings.finding: ADRs 0026-0029 referenced throughout the plan as 'the governing spec' do not exist in docs/adr/. The directory tops out at 0026 (autodeploy-on-box-timer.md — an unrelated ADR about systemd deploy timers). ADRs 0027, 0028, and 0029 are entirely absent. Additionally, the plan's 'ADR 0026' about reconciler baseline conflicts with the existing 0026 about auto-deploy.
findings.location: Summary: 'All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST'; Implementation contract: 'ADRs 0026–0029 are committed under docs/adr/ on this branch'
findings.suggested_fix: Since 0026 is taken, number the new reconciler ADRs starting at 0027 (baseline), 0028 (classify/ObservedJira), 0029 (circuit breaker/census), 0030 (replay tests), and update all plan references accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm ADRs 0026–0029 (the reconciler decision-matrix, per-binding baseline, circuit-breaker, and ObservedJira four-way classification specs) are committed and accessible in `docs/adr/` before implementation begins, or inline the governing invariants directly into this ticket so the spec is self-contained.
findings.finding: The plan's governing specs (ADRs 0026–0029) are cited as committed to docs/adr/ on this branch, but the repo's docs/adr/ directory only contains files up to 0026 (which is about auto-deploy, unrelated to this feature). ADRs 0027, 0028, and 0029 do not exist in the repo, so the invariants the plan says to 'read first' are unreachable.
findings.location: Summary section: 'All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit mapping table in the Implementation Contract (or in classify.py's module docstring) that enumerates which existing signal type (ProbeBranch member, _DELETED sentinel, snapshot-miss) maps to each ObservedJira state, including the ABSENT_IN_WINDOW origin rule (snapshot miss vs. probe result).
findings.finding: The plan references a mapping from existing codebase signal types (outbound_differ._DELETED/_TRANSPORT_ERROR sentinels and inbound_probe.ProbeBranch enum values) to the new ObservedJira enum states, but never enumerates the mapping rules. The Implementation Contract says 'Raw GET outcomes are mapped to ObservedJira by the caller' but does not specify which ProbeBranch variants (PRESENT_RESOLVED, PRESENT_FILTERED, ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED, UNREACHABLE) map to which ObservedJira states, nor whether ABSENT_IN_WINDOW originates from snapshot-miss vs. a probe outcome. This protocol is needed by the matrix but is not co-located with the ObservedJira schema definition.
findings.location: Implementation Contract section: '`jira` parameter type' + 'Grace-met for RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE'; existing code: inbound_probe.py ProbeBranch, outbound_differ.py _DELETED/_TRANSPORT_ERROR
findings.suggested_fix: Add a co-located mapping block: `ABSENT_IN_WINDOW` = key missing from JQL snapshot (no direct GET performed yet this pass); `CONFIRMED_404` = _safe_get_issue returned _DELETED (HTTPError 404) OR ProbeBranch.ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED; `TRANSPORT_ERROR` = _safe_get_issue returned _TRANSPORT_ERROR OR ProbeBranch.UNREACHABLE; `PRESENT` = 200 response / ProbeBranch.PRESENT_RESOLVED or PRESENT_FILTERED (both alive).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete invocation trigger for `seed_baselines_from_snapshot` (e.g., a one-time migration script, a CLI command, or a reconcile-startup guard) and document whether it is idempotent across deploys.
findings.finding: The CREATE trigger for seed_baselines_from_snapshot is unspecified: the plan introduces the function but never says who calls it, when (migration step, CLI invocation, first reconcile pass?), or what prevents it from never being called in production — leaving all bindings perpetually in local-wins degraded mode.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section — 'One-shot seed helper seed_baselines_from_snapshot'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-time migration entry point (e.g., `rebar migrate seed-baselines --snapshot <path>`) that loads the frozen snapshot and calls seed_baselines_from_snapshot, and document that it must be run before the first reconcile pass that consumes baselines. Alternatively, add a startup guard in reconcile.py that detects zero-baseline-count and logs a WARNING, directing operators to run the seed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit ADRs 0027, 0028, and 0029 to `docs/adr/` (or renumber them to the correct existing ADR numbers) before implementation begins, and correct the Summary + Implementation Contract cross-references so the matrix cell decisions and baseline decision points are traceable to the actual committed ADR text.
findings.finding: The plan's Summary gates the entire design on 'read ADRs 0026–0029 (docs/adr/) FIRST', but three of those four ADRs (0027, 0028, 0029) do not exist anywhere in the repository, and the one that does exist (0026) is about an unrelated autodeploy topic. An executor cannot read the governing specs they are directed to, and the Implementation Contract cites ADR 0026 decision points and ADR 0028 regression cells as binding constraints on the matrix, making this a critical capability gap.
findings.location: Summary section: 'All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST'; Implementation Contract section: 'grace met per ADR 0026 #2', 'the ADR 0028 regression cells'
findings.suggested_fix: If the ADRs are being written as part of this epic, add them as a prerequisite deliverable of THIS ticket (they are listed as 'on this branch' in the Summary). Alternatively, if the governing decisions are in a different document or ticket, update all cross-references to point to the correct location. Verify that the correct ADR number for 'per-binding baseline 4 decision points' is actually 0027 or 0028 (not 0026 which is autodeploy) and fix all plan references accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either inline the ADR content sufficient to execute the ticket (the Implementation Contract already does much of this), or add an explicit task 'Commit ADRs 0027–0029 to docs/adr/ as the first step of this ticket' with enough content specified here to be self-contained without the pre-read.
findings.finding: ADRs 0026–0029 are referenced as the governing specification ('All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST') and the Implementation Contract states they are 'committed under docs/adr/ on this branch.' However, docs/adr/ only contains files up to 0026-autodeploy-on-box-timer.md (an unrelated ADR), and ADRs 0027–0029 do not exist anywhere in the repo. The plan cannot be executed as written because the primary specification documents are absent. The plan's own Implementation Contract acknowledges these are to-be-written ('on this branch') but the ticket body instructs executors to 'read them FIRST' — creating an instruction-locality gap where the spec is cited as pre-existing but is actually a deliverable of this same ticket.
findings.location: Summary section: 'All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST'; Implementation Contract: 'ADRs 0026–0029 are committed under docs/adr/ on this branch (the governing spec)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the dual-write shadow-phase protocol: document exactly what `reconciler.baseline_dual_write=True` causes (e.g., write baseline on every `bind_confirm` from the live snapshot), and add an AC asserting that any binding confirmed AFTER `seed_baselines_from_snapshot` is called also receives a baseline entry (either via `seed` re-run or via `bind_confirm` side-effect).
findings.finding: The plan registers `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` 'for the rollout's shadow phase' but never specifies the dual-write protocol: which path receives the old write vs. the new during shadow mode, what happens to binding entries created or modified BETWEEN `seed_baselines_from_snapshot` execution and classifier activation, and whether those entries are guaranteed to receive a baseline before the circuit breaker is armed. Without this, there is a silent window where post-seed new bindings have no baseline and silently fall back to local-wins with no alerting.
findings.location: Implementation contract — resolved specifics / `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` bullet; Per-binding baseline section / `seed_baselines_from_snapshot`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `seed_baselines_from_snapshot` spec: 'When `baseline_dual_write=True`, `bind_confirm` also calls `set_baseline` from the caller-supplied current snapshot (or sets a sentinel indicating re-seed needed).' Add a test in `test_binding_baseline.py` that confirms a binding created AFTER seeding also acquires a baseline without requiring a second manual seed invocation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the caller of classify() must resolve `RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE` (via the same `_env_int` helper or a shared config accessor) and pass it as the `grace` kwarg, so the prediction and the note_absent enaction use the same threshold.
findings.finding: The classify() grace-threshold default (grace=3) can silently disagree with the live enactor (note_absent reads RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE from the environment). If the env var is set to a value other than 3, classify() will predict PROBE_GET while note_absent fires RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE (or vice versa), breaking the predict→enact invariant the plan relies on for correctness. The plan says the caller of classify() is 'the live probe path', but does not specify that the caller must resolve the env var and pass it as the grace kwarg to align the prediction with the enaction.
findings.location: Implementation contract — resolved specifics / Grace-met for RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE section; binding_store.py:note_absent (lines 292-312)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level helper `_resolve_grace() -> int` (mirroring binding_store._env_int) in classify.py or expose `binding_store.ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE_DEFAULT` as a public constant, and document in the Implementation Contract that callers must pass `grace=_resolve_grace()` to align with the enactor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `classify()`'s `grace` arg is sourced in production callers — either it reads `RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE` (keeping one source of truth) or the env var is deprecated in favour of the config key, and document this reconciliation explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a `grace` keyword arg to `classify()` (default 3) for predicting RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE, while the existing `binding_store.note_absent()` reads `RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE` from an env var (also default 3). Two independent grace-period sources now exist for the same lifecycle decision; the plan does not justify this split or specify which source of truth wins when they disagree.
findings.location: Implementation contract: 'classify(local, jira, binding, baseline, *, grace: int = 3)' / 'classify PREDICTS whether this miss crosses grace using binding["absent_404_count"] + 1 >= grace; binding_store.note_absent remains the sole ENACTOR'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `'max_acting_fraction': lambda v, k: _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0)` and `'baseline_dual_write': lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k)` to the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer dict in `_config_schema.py` (the ReconcilerConfig dataclass addition alone is insufficient).
findings.finding: The plan says to register `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` in `_config_schema.py ReconcilerConfig`, but omits the parallel edit required to the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer dict. Without a coercer entry, `coerce_sparse()` will silently warn-and-drop these keys when reading from TOML, so `Config.from_mapping({'reconciler': {'max_acting_fraction': 0.05}})` ignores the override entirely.
findings.location: Implementation contract: 'registered in _config_schema.py ReconcilerConfig as float = 0.10, coercer _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0)'
findings.suggested_fix:
In `_config_schema.py` at the `'reconciler'` block (~line 436), add:
`'max_acting_fraction': lambda v, k: _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0),
'baseline_dual_write': lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k),`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly call out that both `ReconcilerConfig` dataclass fields AND the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercion table entries must be added for `max_acting_fraction` (float coercer) and `baseline_dual_write` (bool coercer) — a two-place schema change, not one.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` will be 'registered in `_config_schema.py` `ReconcilerConfig`', but the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercion table (the two-place schema: dataclass field + coercion entry) does not include these keys. New keys added only to the dataclass but not to `_SECTIONS` are silently ignored on TOML parse and warned as unknown — the coercer is a verified, mandatory second step.
findings.location: Implementation contract section: 'reconciler.max_acting_fraction — registered in _config_schema.py ReconcilerConfig as float = 0.10, coercer _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0). A reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False key is added alongside'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `_SECTIONS['reconciler']`: `'max_acting_fraction': lambda v, k: _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0)` and `'baseline_dual_write': lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k)` alongside the dataclass field additions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a short ADR section (or CLAUDE.md note) documenting the `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` flag: what dual-write means, when it is safe to enable, and its planned removal trigger — even a stub pointing to the rollout task is sufficient.
findings.finding: The `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` config key is registered in this ticket 'for the rollout's shadow phase,' but no ADR, design doc, or CLAUDE.md entry documents the dual-write/shadow-write pattern it gates, nor the conditions under which it would be flipped. This leaves future maintainers with an undocumented feature flag whose semantics and lifecycle are opaque.
findings.location: Implementation contract — resolved specifics / `reconciler.baseline_dual_write`
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/adr/0026-baseline.md (or a new ADR 0030), add a 'Shadow phase / dual-write' sub-section stating: the flag enables write-through of baseline fields to both old and new storage paths; it is enabled during the rollout task's canary phase; it is removed once 100% of bindings carry a baseline and old-path reads are retired.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that when BreakerVerdict.allowed is False, the caller MUST emit a log/metric (e.g., SyncLogger.log('circuit_breaker_tripped', **verdict_fields)) — either document this as a caller contract here or include it in the census() output and assert it in the unit tests.
findings.finding: When check_blast_radius returns BreakerVerdict(allowed=False), no observability (log line, metric, or alert) is specified for this failure mode, meaning a production circuit-breaker trip would be silent to operators.
findings.location: Circuit breaker section + Implementation contract — BreakerVerdict definition
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the decision_census record shape a top-level 'breaker_tripped' boolean, and in the census() docstring specify that callers must emit a WARNING-level log (or equivalent metric) when breaker_tripped is True. Add an assertion to test_frozen_census.py that census() with a synthetic over-threshold input produces a record with breaker_tripped=True.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the caller of `classify()` always passes `grace=` sourced from the same config/env resolution used by `binding_store.note_absent` (e.g. via `_env_int('RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE', 3, minimum=1)`), or export `_DEFAULT_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE` from `binding_store.py` and import it as the default in `classify.py` to keep a single source of truth.
findings.finding: The `grace: int = 3` default in `classify()`'s keyword-only signature duplicates the `_DEFAULT_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE = 3` constant already defined in `binding_store.py`, creating two independent sources of truth for the same operational threshold.
findings.location: New module section — `classify.py`; Implementation contract — grace-met bullet: `classify(local, jira, binding, baseline, *, grace: int = 3)`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item (or extend AC item 3) to require `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` registered in `_config_schema.py` alongside `max_acting_fraction`.
findings.finding: The Implementation Contract introduces `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` as a new config key 'for the rollout's shadow phase' but no Acceptance Criterion covers its existence, registration in _config_schema.py, or default value. It is described work with no verifying criterion.
findings.location: Implementation Contract section ('A reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False key is added alongside') vs Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC item 3 to: 'check_blast_radius + census implemented with unit tests; config keys `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (default 0.10) AND `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` (default False) registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Implementation Contract's sentinel attribution: `_safe_get_issue` and its sentinels (`_DELETED`, `_TRANSPORT_ERROR`) live in `outbound_differ.py`, not `inbound_probe`; the ObservedJira mapping adapter must import from `outbound_differ` (or be co-located there), not from `inbound_probe`.
findings.finding: The Implementation Contract states '`inbound_probe`/`_safe_get_issue` sentinels → `CONFIRMED_404`/`TRANSPORT_ERROR`' as if `_safe_get_issue` is in or adjacent to `inbound_probe`. In reality `_safe_get_issue` is a module-level function in `outbound_differ.py`, returning the sentinel objects `_DELETED` and `_TRANSPORT_ERROR` defined there — not in `inbound_probe`.
findings.location: Implementation Contract section: 'Raw GET outcomes are mapped to ObservedJira by the caller (the live probe path; inbound_probe/_safe_get_issue sentinels → CONFIRMED_404/TRANSPORT_ERROR)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/__init__.py` (empty, matching the existing subdirectory convention) to the plan's list of new files to create.
findings.finding: The five new test files are planned under `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/` (a non-existent directory), but the plan does not mention creating the required `__init__.py` for that new package. Every existing test subdirectory in the repo (apply/, conflict/, diffing/, fetch/, integration_gates/, mutate/, orchestrate/, state/) contains an `__init__.py`.
findings.location: Tests section: 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/test_matrix.py', 'test_properties.py', 'test_frozen_census.py', 'test_multipass_stateful.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Create an empty `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/__init__.py` alongside the four test files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving step for the config-schema registration in AC3 — either name a test (e.g. `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_config_schema.py`) that asserts `ReconcilerConfig` exposes `max_acting_fraction: float = 0.10` and `baseline_dual_write: bool = False`, or reference the existing schema-validation test that would catch their absence.
findings.finding: AC3 requires that `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write`) be registered in `_config_schema.py`, but no named test file or proving command is specified to verify that registration. The only proving command (`make test`) would pass even if the keys were omitted from the schema, as long as the breaker/census unit tests use the default value directly.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC3: 'config key reconciler.max_acting_fraction (default 0.10) registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `test_config_typed.py` to the plan's edit list and include assertions for `c.reconciler.max_acting_fraction == 0.10` and `c.reconciler.baseline_dual_write is False` in the defaults test, and a round-trip coercion test for both new keys.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate `test_config_typed.py` (tests/unit/test_config_typed.py) as a file requiring updates. That test asserts specific `ReconcilerConfig` defaults (e.g. `c.reconciler.lock_max_retries == 5`, `c.reconciler.id_guard_bypass_unsafe is False`) and will need a new assertion for `max_acting_fraction == 0.10` and `baseline_dual_write is False` to verify the new keys are wired correctly end-to-end.
findings.location: AC3: 'check_blast_radius + census implemented with unit tests; config key reconciler.max_acting_fraction (default 0.10) registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write`) to the operator-facing config reference (README config table, runbook, or CLAUDE.md) with their defaults, valid ranges, and when to adjust them.
findings.finding: The two new operator-tunable config keys (`reconciler.max_acting_fraction`, `reconciler.baseline_dual_write`) are registered in the typed schema but the plan makes no mention of updating any operator-facing documentation (README, runbook, or CLAUDE.md config reference) to make them discoverable to operators who tune reconciler behavior.
findings.location: AC line: 'config key `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (default 0.10) registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` from this ticket's `_config_schema.py` changes and defer it to the rollout task that actually consumes it.
findings.finding: `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` is added to `_config_schema.py:ReconcilerConfig` in this ticket but has zero consumers in this ticket; its only stated purpose is 'the rollout's shadow phase' which is explicitly a separate downstream task.
findings.location: Implementation contract section — `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` bullet; AC line: `reconciler.max_acting_fraction (default 0.10) registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py)`
findings.suggested_fix: Add `baseline_dual_write` to `ReconcilerConfig` only in the rollout task's PR, alongside the first code that reads it. This ticket needs only `max_acting_fraction`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the conflict between AC4's 'hypothesis suite' wording and the Implementation Contract's 'stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis' mandate — replace 'hypothesis suite' in AC4 with 'seeded-random property suite' or equivalent unambiguous phrasing.
findings.finding: AC4 uses the phrase 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties', but the Implementation Contract explicitly forbids the hypothesis library ('Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis — the repo declares no hypothesis dep'). An executor reading AC4 literally would install and use hypothesis, directly contradicting the contract.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4 vs Implementation Contract section 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis'
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4 to read: 'seeded-random property suite (stdlib random, no hypothesis dep) includes the four named properties: convergence, no-orphan, conservation, direction-preservation' — matching the Implementation Contract exactly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC item 4 to replace 'hypothesis suite' with 'seeded-random property suite (stdlib random)' so the criterion matches the implementation contract and can be verified without the hypothesis library.
findings.finding: The AC says 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' but the Implementation Contract explicitly forbids the hypothesis library ('Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis — the repo declares no hypothesis dep and no test uses it'). A verifier checking the AC would look for hypothesis library imports and fail the criterion incorrectly.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 4 vs Implementation Contract section 'Property tests use stdlib random'
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC item 4 to: 'All five test files pass; frozen-census fixture checked in (sanitized); seeded-random property suite (stdlib `random`, no hypothesis dep) includes the four named properties (convergence, no-orphan, conservation, direction-preservation); the multi-pass test persists baseline through a real file and asserts self-heal after injected partial failure.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly list `tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/bindings.json`, `snapshot.json`, and `locals.json` as concrete deliverables in the plan's file list and AC4, and confirm the generation script or generation process is documented.
findings.finding: The plan names `tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/` as a new fixture directory to be checked in, but does not enumerate it as a concrete edit target in the plan's file list or AC, making it easy to omit from the PR. The directory does not currently exist.
findings.location: Tests section: 'frozen fixtures (tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/: bindings.json + snapshot skeleton, PII/body-stripped) asserting EXACTLY 7 acting decisions'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'hypothesis suite' in AC4 with 'seeded-random property suite' (or equivalent non-library phrasing) to match the Implementation Contract's explicit prohibition on the Hypothesis library.
findings.finding: AC4 says 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' but the Implementation Contract explicitly says 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis — the repo declares no hypothesis dep and no test uses it; adding one would drift CI.' These two statements directly contradict each other within the same plan. An executor reading AC4 in isolation would interpret 'hypothesis suite' as referring to the Hypothesis property-based-testing library and could add it as a dependency.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4; Implementation contract — 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis'
findings.suggested_fix: AC4: change '…hypothesis suite includes the four named properties…' to '…seeded-random property suite (stdlib random, no Hypothesis library) includes the four named properties…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the AC wording with the Implementation Contract: replace 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' with 'seeded-random property suite (stdlib `random`, no `hypothesis` dep) includes the four named properties'.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria say 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' while the Implementation Contract explicitly forbids hypothesis ('Property tests use stdlib `random` (seeded), NOT `hypothesis` — the repo declares no `hypothesis` dep and no test uses it'). Both are confirmed by the codebase: pyproject.toml has no hypothesis dependency, and no test file imports it. An executor reading the AC will attempt to install hypothesis; an executor reading the Implementation Contract will not. The AC is the authoritative gate document and it references a prohibited library.
findings.location: AC line 4: 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties'; Implementation Contract: 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis'
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC line: '...seeded-random property suite includes the four named properties (convergence, no-orphan, conservation, direction-preservation)...'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] When adding `max_acting_fraction` and `baseline_dual_write` to `ReconcilerConfig`, also add matching coercer lambdas to the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` dict in `_config_schema.py` — omitting the coercer makes the keys unknown to `coerce_sparse` and triggers a warning/error on load.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (float, default 0.10) and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` (bool, default False) are 'registered in `_config_schema.py` `ReconcilerConfig`', but neither field exists in the current `ReconcilerConfig` dataclass or the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer map.
findings.location: AC line: 'config key `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (default 0.10) registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py)'; Implementation Contract: 'registered in `_config_schema.py` `ReconcilerConfig` as `float = 0.10`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to _SECTIONS['reconciler']: `'max_acting_fraction': lambda v, k: _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0)` and `'baseline_dual_write': lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align terminology: rename 'acting decisions' in the Tests section and AC to 'drift decisions' (or 'actionable decisions') when referring to the 7-count, and reserve 'acting' exclusively for the circuit-breaker subset (count=6), matching the Implementation Contract.
findings.finding: The Tests section and AC item 4 describe the frozen-census test as 'asserting EXACTLY 7 acting decisions', but the Circuit Breaker section defines ACTING as {TERMINAL_TRANSITION, RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE, ADOPT} (explicitly excluding PROBE_GET), and the Implementation Contract states 'census(...).acting_count == 6'. The word 'acting' is used with two different meanings across sections (7 drift/actionable decisions vs 6 circuit-breaker-acting decisions), which would confuse an implementer writing the assertion.
findings.location: Tests section (test_frozen_census.py) vs Circuit Breaker section vs Implementation Contract ('acting_count == 6')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Tests section change to: 'asserting EXACTLY 7 drift decisions (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION, 1x PROBE_GET, 1x ADOPT) then assert census(...).acting_count == 6 (PROBE_GET excluded per breaker definition)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document an explicit rollback procedure for the bindings.json version bump: either (a) confirm that old code ignores unknown version values and unknown keys (expand-contract safe), or (b) provide a one-command downgrade script that strips the baseline fields and resets the version, and add this to the AC.
findings.finding: The plan bumps the `bindings.json` version field but provides no rollback path for the scenario where deployed code must be rolled back to a prior version that does not understand the bumped schema. The prior code's version-check behavior (strict-fail vs ignore-unknown-keys) is unspecified; if it is strict, a rollback would cause all bindings to fail closed, losing all binding state.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section ('bump the file version field') and Acceptance Criteria item 2 ('version bumped with back-compat read')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit version-field reading in `BindingStore._load()` (or a dedicated `_migrate()` helper) so the test for 'version bumped with back-compat read' has something concrete to assert against; a purely cosmetic bump with no code gate cannot satisfy the AC.
findings.finding: The plan states 'bump the file `version` field; older entries without baseline are VALID' but `BindingStore._load()` has no version-parsing or migration logic — it loads whatever JSON is in bindings.json without inspecting the version field. The plan does not specify what mechanism enforces back-compat read at the code level.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section: 'bump the file `version` field; older entries without baseline are VALID (absent baseline degrades to local-wins per ADR 0026 #2)'
findings.suggested_fix: In `_load()`, after `data = json.load(f)`, add: `version = data.get('version', 1)` and assert/migrate if needed; for v2 back-compat, verify that entries without `baseline` are accepted (no-op migration). Document this in the docstring.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
